---
title: "Resources"
---

This section is the research-site equivalent of a well-kept drawer of tools. It works best when it stays selective.

## What belongs here

:::{.stack-grid}
::: {.stack-card}
### Code and analysis

Reusable repositories, notebooks, scripts, and small utilities that support your scientific workflow.
:::
::: {.stack-card}
### Data and protocols

Datasets, metadata notes, experimental protocols, imaging workflows, and processing checklists.
:::
::: {.stack-card}
### Talks and teaching

Slide decks, tutorials, course notes, and public-facing explainers that are worth revisiting.
:::
:::

## Curation rule

Only add resources that you would still recommend to a collaborator six months from now. A short, reliable list is better than a noisy archive.
